title: tabebuia pinetorum
intro: Tabebuia pinetorum, also known as Lapacho or Pau D'Arco, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, due to the presence of naphthoquinones, such as lapachol and β-lapachone. The health benefits of Tabebuia pinetorum include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and fight against various diseases, including cancer and infections. Tabebuia pinetorum can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
